message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: State Fare Bar + Kitchen (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Tripadvisor 258 reviews (4.0, Travelers' Choice); Yelp 285 reviews per May 2026 search snippet; OpenTable cited at 1,199 reviews in one search snippet [UNVERIFIED — OpenTable page timed out]; Google review count not directly verified [UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: Mostly positive but mixed — strong on setting, service, and select dishes (mushroom steak, vegan ramen, scallops, short rib); recurring critiques on inconsistency and resort-level pricing vs. value
- Longevity: Tied to The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage reopening era (early 2010s, following the 'seven years in the making' reopen); remastered in 2024 as part of the resort's multi-year refresh. Operator continuity via Marriott / Ritz-Carlton. [UNVERIFIED — exact original opening year of State Fare not confirmed in a primary source this pass]
- Credentials: ["Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice (top 10%) — per Tripadvisor listing","GAYOT 14/20 score","Palm Springs Life — '6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation' inclusion","Time Out Palm Springs best-restaurants inclusion (per search snippet) [UNVERIFIED — Time Out page not directly retrieved]","Executive Chef Bruno Lopez — Maitres Cuisiniers de France member (chef-level credential, not restaurant-level; verified via mcf-usa.com and CBS feature)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably on 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' and 'date night Rancho Mirage California cuisine' (named in Palm Springs Life, Locale Magazine, visitgreaterpalmsprings, OpenTable fine-dining list). Does NOT surface in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026 California cuisine' results — outranked by Spencer's, Cork Tree, Sweet Basil, The Hideout, Wally's Desert Turtle. Within the Ritz property itself, The Edge Steakhouse tends to dominate the resort-dining narrative over State Fare.
- Site structured data: Canonical page is ritzcarlton.com (Marriott CMS) — clean hours, address, phone, PDF menus, but no visible schema.org Restaurant markup on the dining hub page. Menus are PDF-only (not HTML), opaque to most agents. Marriott property page (marriott.com) returned 403 to WebFetch [UNVERIFIED on structured-data presence]. No standalone domain or independent site for State Fare — entirely dependent on Marriott / Ritz CMS for first-party signal.
- Visibility gap score: 4
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about State Fare Bar & Kitchen in Rancho Mirage on TripAdvisor:

## Overall Rating
State Fare Bar & Kitchen is rated 4.0 of 5 on TripAdvisor and ranked #13 of 68 restaurants in Rancho Mirage, with 257 unbiased reviews.

## Review Highlights

**Positive Reviews:**
- Visitors praised the breakfast and bar snacks as delicious, with great service and views.
- One reviewer highlighted exceptional server service, noting staff who "went above and beyond."
- Anniversary diners complimented the magical setting, amazing food, attentive staff who accommodated dietary restrictions, and an engaging server.

**Mixed/Negative Reviews:**
- One complaint mentioned that online menus weren't updated, with unavailable items, and the lobster roll and veggie wrap were described as mediocre.
- Another reviewer noted expensive pricing with "just OK" quality for items like short ribs and Arctic char.
- Some guests found the menu limited with a bar-focused selection and pricing to be very expensive.

The restaurant, located at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage, appears to offer a scenic setting with generally positive feedback on service and breakfast offerings, though pricing and menu variety were occasional concerns.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Rancho Mirage for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Options

**The Edge Steakhouse**
Located at The Ritz-Carlton, The Edge Steakhouse features sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list.

**Wally's Desert Turtle**
Established in 1978 by renowned restaurateur Wally Botello, Wally's Desert Turtle is celebrated as the pioneer of fine dining in the desert. This longtime Rancho Mirage favorite offers a refined, unhurried dining experience rooted in classic desert style, with curated wines, nightly entertainment and the Sahara Lounge where happy hour runs all night.

**Willie's Modern Fare**
Restaurateur Willie Rhine pairs five-star hospitality with fine dining elegance, offering classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**Foxy's Kitchen + Bar**
One of the newer names on Rancho Mirage's dining scene, this family-run spot features steaks and osso buco as favorites, complemented by seafood, pastas, and vegan options.

**Si Bon**
Si Bon offers a fusion of French and Belgian cuisine with a charming atmosphere, featuring classic French and Belgian dishes expertly prepared with creativity and flair.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about State Fare Bar at the Ritz Carlton Rancho Mirage:

State Fare Bar & Kitchen features a wraparound terrace, indoor-outdoor bar, firepits, and a new house-made pasta menu led by chef Bruno Lopez.

Chef Bruno Lopez is a member of the elite Maîtres Cuisiniers de France, which admits only the world's top-ranked chefs to its 350-member ranks. He trained at the Ferrandi School in Paris and apprenticed at L'Espadon, a Michelin one-star restaurant at Hotel Ritz in Paris, before honing his culinary skills in France and South America.

State Fare Bar & Kitchen was remastered in 2024 as a modern California dining destination that celebrates the vibrant and diverse flavors of the region. The restaurant presents daily breakfast, lunch and dinner as well as Saturday and Sunday Brunch, offering multiple gathering spots, a cozy fireplace "snug," a private dining room, indoor/outdoor seating, panoramic terraces and a chef's display kitchen.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent date night restaurant options in Rancho Mirage for 2026:

## Fine Dining Options

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - A Rancho Mirage institution that delivers timeless elegance with refined California cuisine, glamorous setting with mirrored walls, soft lighting, and crystal accents.

**The Edge Steakhouse** (The Ritz-Carlton) - Located within The Ritz-Carlton with sweeping views of Rancho Mirage, crafts remarkable wagyu steaks and seafood paired with an international wine list.

## Newer/Notable Restaurants

**Double Date** - A new dining experience at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa by executive chef Jose Lopez, inspired by the Coachella Valley's reputation as the 'date capital of the world'. Double Date is open daily for dinner from 5 to 10 p.m.

## Other Date-Friendly Options

**Si Bon Belgian Bistro** - A minimalist bistro with a French-Belgian menu, offering European flavors at breakfast, lunch and dinner.

**Shabu Shabu Zen** - A cozy Japanese dining experience where you can make conversation while sipping fresh, aromatic plum sake and indulging in premium cuts of tender Wagyu beef and fresh seafood.

**Wally's Desert Turtle** also participates in Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week with specially curated 3-course dinner menus.

content.content: No, State Fare Bar + Kitchen is not listed in this article. The six restaurants featured for Rancho Mirage date nights are: Wally's Desert Turtle, The Edge Steakhouse, Shame on the Moon, BluEmber, Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine, and Shabu Shabu Zen and Sake Bar.

# The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage: Dining Information

## On-Property Restaurants

The hotel operates three dining venues:

1. **The Edge Steakhouse** - A mountaintop fine dining establishment described as "the Coachella Valley's only mountaintop culinary destination" offering dry-aged steaks with valley views.
   - Hours: Thursday-Sunday, 5:00 PM-10:00 PM (closed Mon-Wed)
   - Dress Code: Smart Casual
   - Phone: +1 760-202-6180

2. **State Fare Bar & Kitchen** - An American restaurant featuring seasonal California ingredients and local wines.
   - Hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 7:00 AM-10:00 PM; Fri-Sat 7:00 AM-11:00 PM
   - Dress Code: Casual
   - Offers breakfast, lunch, and dinner menus (available as PDFs)

3. **Air Pool Bar** - A casual poolside venue serving burgers, salads, and cocktails.
   - Hours: Daily, 10:00 AM-5:00 PM
   - Dress Code: Casual

## Booking & Contact

Reservations can be made through OpenTable or by calling the main hotel line (+1 760-321-8282). Room service is also available.

## Special Offerings

The property features private dining options and exclusive culinary experiences with Master Chef Bruno on select evenings.

The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage opened on May 15, 2014, and StateFare Bar & Kitchen is the main restaurant at the Ritz Carlton Rancho Mirage.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about State Fare and its recognition in Time Out's best restaurants:

## State Fare Bar & Kitchen

Time Out highlights Palm Springs' best restaurants, including State Fare Bar & Kitchen at The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage, noting seasonal California fare, seafood linguine, and a refined setting with valley views.

### About the Restaurant

Remastered in 2024, State Fare Bar & Kitchen is a modern California dining destination featuring fresh ingredients with a seasonally inspired menu offering artisanal dishes and curated cocktails that highlight the best of Southern California's culinary fare.

State Fare Bar & Kitchen at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage presents a fresh take on California cuisine, drawing visitors and locals alike for breakfast, lunch and dinner.

### Dining Experience

State Fare Bar & Kitchen features a wraparound terrace, indoor-outdoor bar, firepits, and a new house-made pasta menu led by chef Bruno Lopez. Guests can also choose to dine on the terrace, which has heat lamps and fire pits, in a dog-friendly section that looks out on the pool and palm trees with the mountains in the distance.

content.text: Major finding: The Ritz-Carlton dining page DOES contain schema.org JSON-LD with a Restaurant entity for State Fare Bar & Kitchen — contradicting the claim that there is "no visible schema.org Restaurant markup on the dining hub page."

confirms: Tripadvisor rating of 4.0 with review count near 258 (Tripadvisor shows 257 reviews, ranked #13 of 68 in Rancho Mirage) — within rounding of the claim
Yelp 285 reviews confirmed in Yelp result snippet (May 2026 update)
GAYOT 14/20 score confirmed via GAYOT.com listing
Palm Springs Life '6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation' inclusion appears in indexed results (the article 403'd to WebFetch but is indexed under that title with State Fare association)
Chef Bruno Lopez Maitres Cuisiniers de France membership confirmed (CBS feature and Palm Springs Life corroborate)

Sentiment pattern (positive on setting/service, mixed on inconsistency and pricing) reflected in Tripadvisor review excerpts (mentions of 'just OK' short ribs, 'very expensive', mediocre lobster roll alongside praise for service)
Surfaces on 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' and 'date night Rancho Mirage' query families (appears via OpenTable, visitgreaterpalmsprings, Tripadvisor listings)
Does NOT surface in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026 California cuisine' results (Cork Tree, Sweet Basil, The Hideout dominate as claimed)

Within Ritz property narrative, The Edge Steakhouse leads (confirmed by Ritz-Carlton's own dining page billing The Edge as 'mountaintop culinary destination' and date-night articles featuring The Edge over State Fare)
Menus are PDF-only on Ritz-Carlton page (no HTML menu rendering)
Original opening tied to Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage 2014 reopening (resort reopened May 15, 2014) — refining the 'early 2010s' claim
contradicts: CLAIM: 'no visible schema.org Restaurant markup on the dining hub page.' ACTUAL: The ritzcarlton.com dining page DOES include JSON-LD with @type Hotel containing a @type Restaurant entity for State Fare Bar & Kitchen with name, description, servesCuisine ('American'), openingHoursSpecification (Mon-Thu/Sun 7:00-22:00, Fri-Sat 7:00-23:00), acceptsReservations (OpenTable URL), and PostalAddress (68900 Frank Sinatra Drive, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270). There is also a FAQPage JSON-LD that names State Fare in answers. This materially weakens the structured-data gap argument and the visibility-gap score should likely be lower than 4.

CLAIM: 'Palm Springs Life — 6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation inclusion' and Locale Magazine inclusion implied via date-night queries. ACTUAL: The Locale Magazine '6 Restaurants in Rancho Mirage Perfect for Your Next Date Night' article does NOT include State Fare — it lists Wally's Desert Turtle, The Edge Steakhouse, Shame on the Moon, BluEmber, Catalan, and Shabu Shabu Zen. State Fare is outranked by The Edge even within Ritz-property date-night coverage in this specific list.

Google review count — not independently verified
Tripadvisor 'Travelers' Choice' badge — Tripadvisor listing confirms 4.0 rating and rank #13 but the Travelers' Choice badge itself was not surfaced in the snippets retrieved
Time Out Palm Springs inclusion — only surfaced as an AI-summarized claim in a search snippet, not directly retrieved from timeout.com
Marriott.com property page structured data — page returned 403 to WebFetch (the canonical ritzcarlton.com page DOES have schema, however)
Exact characterization of 'remastered in 2024' as part of a multi-year resort refresh — only the State Fare remaster is well-sourced; the broader resort-refresh framing is less directly evidenced
input.notes: The most important refutation is structured-data: the Ritz-Carlton dining hub actually does emit JSON-LD with a Restaurant node for State Fare (hours, address, cuisine, reservations URL) plus an FAQPage block — this is meaningfully better than 'no visible schema.org markup' and likely warrants lowering the visibility-gap score from 4. PDF-only menus and absence from broader Coachella-Valley California-cuisine roundups remain real gaps, as does being outshone by The Edge in date-night roundups (Locale Magazine confirms this directly). Longevity claim is now more precise: the property and restaurant both opened May 15, 2014 with the Ritz-Carlton reopening. Credential claims for Bruno Lopez and GAYOT 14/20 hold up; the Time Out and Palm Springs Life mentions are best treated as snippet-level evidence rather than first-party verified at this pass.
